growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: chemolithoautotrophic electron donor; 40 mM in medium, oxidized to sulfate; no specific rate reported
autotrophic carbon source; no specific rate reported

sec.method: end product of complete thiosulfate oxidation; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: T. versutus AL2T grew much faster than T. nitratis ALJ2 (mumax of 0.064/h versus 0.024/h, respectively) at 10 degrees C (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.024 +/- 0.0012 /h (Table 1, T. nitratis ALJ2 at 10C, batch). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.024 = 28.88 h.

up.method: chemolithoautotrophic electron donor; 40 mM in medium, oxidized to sulfate; no specific rate reported
autotrophic carbon source; no specific rate reported

sec.method: end product of complete thiosulfate oxidation; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: this was the opposite at 30 degrees C (mumax of 0.28/h versus 0.43/h) (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.43 +/- 0.094 /h (Table 1, T. nitratis ALJ2 at 30C, batch). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.43 = 1.61 h. This strain grows fastest at 30C and slowest at 10C, opposite trend to AL2T.

up.method: growth-limiting electron donor (thiosulfate-limited chemostat), 40 mM in feed; no specific uptake flux reported
autotrophic carbon source; no specific rate reported

sec.method: main oxidation end product; no specific rate reported
accumulation observed in chemostats at 10C (both strains), not at 30C; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: grown at 10 and 30 degrees C in 500-ml chemostat reactors under continuous cultivation with a dilution rate of 0.010 +/- 0.00027/h ... accumulation of elemental sulfur was observed in the chemostats of both Thioalkalivibrio [strains]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.010 +/- 0.00027 /h; doubling_time = ln2/0.010 = 69.31 h. growth_method=calculated (mu=D). Both strains ran at the same dilution rate; growth-rate differences from batch (Table 1) are absent here by design (fixed D).
